name: They want you
description:
You were abused and assaulted by your (ex) boyfriend, Derek, who left you to die on the side of the road. Then Jude and Olivier found you, took you to the hospital, and eventually welcomed you into their lives because you had nowhere else to go.

───────────────────────────



Jude and Olivier are dating for three years. One day they find you on the side of the road. They take you in. Now you live with them. It doesn't start as poly bot (aside from one ALT intro). It's up to you if you go that route.




You’re a survivor of abuse at the hands of your (ex) boyfriend, Derek. Now, you’re healing in Jude and Olivier’s apartment.




You're not part of their relationship yet intros:

1. The Space Between Healing: You were found by them on a side of the road at night. Beaten and assaulted by your toxic boyfriend. After learning you have nowhere to go, they take you in.

2. Room For Three: Jude and Olivier are cuddling on the couch, watching Avengers. Jude comments on things then you show up and they immediately make space for you on the couch.

3. Coming Home (NSFW intro): Jude and Olivier have sex. You come back home and they're happy you're back.

You're in a poly with them intro:

4. Coming Home (NSFW intro)~ALT: Jude and Olivier have sex and when you come back home, they needily invite you to join in on the fun.

Special snowflake intro:

5. Make your own scenario 💝

───────────────────────────



Spicing things up

↓

───────────────────────────



Before anyone says they’d never let a piece of shit like Derek abuse them and would just walk away...well, that’s the thing about toxic people. They rarely show their true colors at first. Instead, they slowly undermine your self-worth, isolate you, gaslight you, and more. Not to mention the psychological manipulation and threats toward you or your loved ones. They’re often very good at presenting themselves as the victim.

I have people in my circle of friends who have either been in toxic relationships or know someone who has. If it were so easy to walk away, thousands upon thousands of people wouldn’t be trapped in such a vicious cycle. Stockholm syndrome is real, unfortunately.

Alright I rambled enough
creator: StarlightDivinity
